figures.caption: Fig. 1: The proposed C-CoT framework. The model takes multi-view camera images and historical trajectories as input, sequentially performing scene description, critical object identification, and current risk estimation. It then evaluates candidate meta-actions through a structured evaluation tree for counterfactual risk reasoning, finally outputting the optimal meta-action and planned trajectory.
Fig. 2: Two-layer meta-action tree illustrating the short-term (as i ) and long-term (al i) decisions. Each root-to-leaf path forms a complete meta-action (as i , a l i).
TABLE I: Quantitative comparison on the DeepAccident validation set.
